title: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
summary: Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner. If no rival emerges, he could become PM this month.
imageAlt: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
aiSummary: Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner. If no rival emerges, he could become PM this month.
keyPoints: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works
multimedia.caption: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works

fullArticle:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s announcement last month that he plans to step down as prime minister and leader of the Labour Party has set in motion the process to select his successor.

The successful candidate will take charge of the governing party, which holds a strong majority in parliament, and will become the United Kingdom’s seventh prime minister in a decade.

Here’s what you need to know about the Labour leadership selection process, including how it works and who is in the running.

Starmer became prime minister on July 5, 2024, after a landslide Labour election victory following 14 years of Conservative Party rule. He announced plans to resign on June 22 amid growing pressure over painful local election defeats for Labour, domestic policy shifts and Starmer’s appointment...
